title: Domain Password Policy Compliance via Resultant Set of Policy Events

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 8.2.3 Strong password parameters — UC evidences domain password-policy compliance via RSoP events, directly matching the v3.2.1 requirement.

dataSources: `index=windows` `sourcetype="ad:password_policy"` from scripted LDAP dump to HEC
spl:
index=windows sourcetype="ad:password_policy" earliest=-7d
| eval meets_pci=if(minPwdLength>=12 AND pwdHistoryLength>=4 AND complexityRequired="TRUE",1,0)
| where meets_pci=0
| stats latest(_time) as last_report by domain_dn, minPwdLength, pwdHistoryLength, complexityRequired
| sort domain_dn
value: Ingests periodic exports of password policy settings to confirm minimum length, complexity, and history requirements for accounts with access to system components in scope for PCI.
implementation: (1) Schedule PowerShell `Get-ADDefaultDomainPasswordPolicy` hourly to HEC. (2) Map fine-grained password policies per CDE OU if used. (3) Alert GPO team on failure. (4) Store compliant snapshots quarterly.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
